checks.finding: T-001..T-004 are all Risk: medium. Their Scope/Done When sections modify none of the sentinel surfaces (authentication, authorization, payment, PII storage, data migration, externally-visible API contracts) — design.md Protected-File Statement confirms no protected-gate file edit anywhere, and security-spec.md boundary B2 states 'scripts invoked READ-ONLY; zero gate files modified... non-decreasing guarantee.' The new `loop-inventory/v1` registry (T-001) is internal-only, consumed solely by the new suites (design.md Data Plan: 'Existing Data Affected: none'). Each Risk Rationale explicitly cites 'no protected-file edit and no security surface change,' matching risk-classification-policy.md's medium tier ('internal tooling ... refactors with tests'). No over-classification present either (no task is high/critical with only UI/docs/test-only scope).

SKIP: no task carries Risk: high or Risk: critical — T-001..T-004 are all 'Risk: medium' in tasks.md. This check's mandate is scoped to high/critical tasks only, matching reviewer-calibration.md's Cannot-Verify Handling example ('A high-risk verification-path check when no high-risk claim or risk surface is present').

Done-When item counts: T-001=8 (TEST-001/002/003/004/017, crlf/constant-parity, CHANGELOG, acceptance-first evidence), T-002=7, T-003=8, T-004=8 — none exceed the 8-item oversize threshold. Each task title names one coherent, non-sequential deliverable (e.g. 'Create the loop inventory ... and its registration-forcing suite'), consistent with tasks.md Global Constraints' 'one issue = one commit' convention. No fragmentation: no 3+ tasks describe only a single function/file edit with no integration concern — every task delivers a multi-file suite plus registration.

SKIP: all four tasks (T-001..T-004) are themselves test-infrastructure/test-authoring tasks (loop-inventory+suite, loop-driver+smoke, loop-consistency suite, loop-escalation suite); acceptance-tests.md Notes states 'This is test-infrastructure work with no user-facing entry point.' No 'functional (non-test, non-infrastructure)' task exists in tasks.md for this check's scoped surface to apply to. For reference, every AC these tasks implement already embeds a negative self-check/error path (AC-001, AC-002, AC-006, AC-007, AC-008 cap-reached BLOCKED, AC-009 RED differential, AC-010, AC-011 deny-without-approval, AC-013 python3-absent, AC-018 prefix-collision).

acceptance-tests.md's integration-labeled entries genuinely span >=2 components: TEST-005 (fixture builder + REAL validate-review-context-set.sh hash-chain check), TEST-006 (REAL precheck + REAL validator driven across rounds), TEST-008 (four review-loop drivers cross-checked against inventory terminal), TEST-011 (check-quality-gate-cycle-limit.sh + select-agent-model.sh + check-terminal-tier-resume.sh). Every TEST-NNN row (TEST-001..TEST-018) maps 1:1 to a specific AC-NNN in the Acceptance Criterion column. No test is mislabeled unit/e2e/acceptance against a mismatched scope.

SKIP: this check's mandate applies only to Risk: high/critical tasks, and none exist (T-001..T-004 are all Risk: medium). Non-blocking note: each task nonetheless carries an explicit `Rollback:` field (T-001..T-004: 'revert this task's commit; nothing protected is touched, so no human-copy re-copy step exists in the rollback path'), exceeding the minimum.

T-001..T-004 all edit the shared files tests/run-all.sh, tests/run-all.ps1, .github/workflows/test.yml (and CHANGELOG.md), but tasks.md Global Constraints documents the edits as 'line-additive and disjoint' with ordering 'STRUCTURALLY enforced by the task graph': Blockers form a strict total order T-001->T-002->T-003->T-004 (T-002 Blockers=T-001; T-003 Blockers=T-001,T-002; T-004 Blockers=T-001,T-002,T-003), resolving the round-1 finding that T-004 previously lacked a T-003 blocker. Global Constraints also explicitly clarifies design.md's 'A3 and A4 in parallel' note is scoped to 'fixture non-collision only,' distinguishing it from this shared-file commit-order constraint.

Every Blockers edge cites a genuine reason. T-002<-T-001: 'the driver reads the committed inventory for assert_artifacts_schema/assert_terminal.' T-003<-T-001,T-002: 'consumes the inventory terminal fields and the loop driver's fixture/round/assert functions.' T-004<-T-001,T-002,T-003: T-004's own Depends On line self-documents that the T-003 edge is 'additionally blocked-on solely to serialize the shared registration-file commits ... T-004's test content itself depends only on T-001/T-002' — a transparently-declared coordination blocker (not a fabricated content dependency), consistent with design.md's Dependency Order note that A3/A4 fixtures 'do not share fixtures at runtime.' No spurious blockers found; no missing content-dependency blockers found.

SKIP: no bugfix or debugging task in scope. T-003 authors a regression-LOCKING suite with a one-time RED differential against 2d8c6a5^, but requirements.md Non-goals states 'Re-fixing the #143 gate contradiction: the fix is already at HEAD (2d8c6a5); this feature only regression-locks it' — T-003 is test-authoring, not a bugfix/debugging task.
